[{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2932319680","pull_request_review_id":3945310491,"id":2932319680,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk86ux6XA","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+{
+  "statusLine": {
+    "command": "input=$(cat); cwd=$(echo \"$input\" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir'); branch=$(git -C \"$cwd\" --no-optional-locks branch --show-current 2\u003e/dev/null); if [ -n \"$branch\" ]; then echo \"cosmo ($branch)\"; else echo \"cosmo\"; fi",","path":".claude/settings.json","commit_id":"ab3751f723fa6de2c629a90f6e1ccdf92544c71d","original_commit_id":"ab3751f723fa6de2c629a90f6e1ccdf92544c71d","user":{"login":"gemini-code-assist[bot]","id":176961590,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCow4Ng","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/956858?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/gemini-code-assist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The shell command can be made more robust. If the `jq` command fails to parse the input and extract `.workspace.current_dir`, the `cwd` variable will be empty. This causes `git -C "$cwd"` to become `git -C ""`, which defaults to running in the current working directory of the process executing the command. This is likely not the intended behavior and could lead to displaying a branch from an unrelated project.

To fix this, you should ensure the `git` command is only executed if `cwd` is successfully populated.

```suggestion
    "command": "input=$(cat); cwd=$(echo \"$input\" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir'); branch=$([ -n \"$cwd\" ] && git -C \"$cwd\" --no-optional-locks branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n \"$branch\" ]; then echo \"cosmo ($branch)\"; else echo \"cosmo\"; fi",
```","created_at":"2026-03-13T16:20:04Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T16:20:04Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/324#discussion_r2932319680","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/324","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2932319680"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/324#discussion_r2932319680"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/324"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2932319680/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":3,"original_line":3,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":3,"position":3,"subject_type":"line"}]

input.command:
git add .claude/settings.json && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Guard git command against empty cwd in status line

Add a check that cwd is non-empty before running git -C, preventing
fallback to an unrelated directory if jq fails to parse input.
EOF
)"

content.text:
All 10 CI checks are now passing:

- **build-home** (4 configs): all pass
- **build-nixos** (3 configs): all pass
- **check**: pass
- **fmt**: pass
- **zizmor**: pass

**Summary of actions taken:**
- Addressed the gemini-code-assist review comment by adding a `[ -n "$cwd" ]` guard before `git -C` in `.claude/settings.json`, preventing fallback to an unrelated directory if `jq` fails to parse input.
- Committed as `4a385c7` and pushed.
- Left a PR comment noting the fix.

PR #324 is green and ready for merge.
